creator: dfilter@FreeBSD.ORG
text:
Author: lippe
Date: Fri Mar 29 00:55:17 2013
New Revision: 315511
URL: http://svnweb.freebsd.org/changeset/ports/315511

Log:
  Qtile is simple, small, and extensible. It's easy to write your own layouts,
  widgets, and built-in commands.
  
  Qtile is written and configured entirely in Python, which means you can
  leverage the full power and flexibility of the language to make it fit your
  needs.
  
  WWW: http://qtile.org
  
  PR:		ports/177448
  Submitted by:	William Grzybowski <william88@gmail.com>

Added:
  head/x11-wm/qtile/
  head/x11-wm/qtile/Makefile   (contents, props changed)
  head/x11-wm/qtile/distinfo   (contents, props changed)
  head/x11-wm/qtile/pkg-descr   (contents, props changed)
  head/x11-wm/qtile/pkg-plist   (contents, props changed)
Modified:
  head/x11-wm/Makefile   (contents, props changed)

Modified: head/x11-wm/Makefile
==============================================================================
--- head/x11-wm/Makefile	Fri Mar 29 00:52:44 2013	(r315510)
+++ head/x11-wm/Makefile	Fri Mar 29 00:55:17 2013	(r315511)
@@ -133,6 +133,7 @@
     SUBDIR += plwm
     SUBDIR += pwm
     SUBDIR += qlwm
+    SUBDIR += qtile
     SUBDIR += ratmen
     SUBDIR += ratmenu
     SUBDIR += ratpoison

Added: head/x11-wm/qtile/Makefile
==============================================================================
--- /dev/null	00:00:00 1970	(empty, because file is newly added)
+++ head/x11-wm/qtile/Makefile	Fri Mar 29 00:55:17 2013	(r315511)
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+# Created by: William Grzybowski <william88@gmail.com>
+# $FreeBSD$
+
+PORTNAME=	qtile
+PORTVERSION=	0.5
+CATEGORIES=	x11-wm
+
+MAINTAINER=	william88@gmail.com
+COMMENT=	A small, flexible, scriptable tiling window manager written in Python
+
+LICENSE=	MIT
+
+RUN_DEPENDS=	${PYTHON_PKGNAMEPREFIX}xpyb>0:${PORTSDIR}/x11/xpyb \
+		${PYTHON_PKGNAMEPREFIX}cairo>0:${PORTSDIR}/graphics/py-cairo
+
+USE_GITHUB=	yes
+GH_ACCOUNT=	qtile
+GH_COMMIT=	dd52b52
+GH_TAGNAME=	v0.5
+
+USE_PYTHON=	yes
+USE_PYDISTUTILS=	easy_install
+
+PORTDOCS=	CHANGELOG INSTALL.rst LICENSE README.rst TODO.rst
+
+.include <bsd.port.options.mk>
+
+post-install:
+.if ${PORT_OPTIONS:MDOCS}
+	@${MKDIR} ${DOCSDIR}
+. for f in ${PORTDOCS}
+	@${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/${f} ${DOCSDIR}
+. endfor
+.endif
+
+.include <bsd.port.mk>

Added: head/x11-wm/qtile/distinfo
==============================================================================
--- /dev/null	00:00:00 1970	(empty, because file is newly added)
+++ head/x11-wm/qtile/distinfo	Fri Mar 29 00:55:17 2013	(r315511)
@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@
+SHA256 (qtile-0.5.tar.gz) = c5a6461bcf5ac79ecb9760c1eee983c325e6940c015bc40706d329df5725cc61
+SIZE (qtile-0.5.tar.gz) = 317109

Added: head/x11-wm/qtile/pkg-descr
==============================================================================
--- /dev/null	00:00:00 1970	(empty, because file is newly added)
+++ head/x11-wm/qtile/pkg-descr	Fri Mar 29 00:55:17 2013	(r315511)
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+Qtile is simple, small, and extensible. It's easy to write your own layouts,
+widgets, and built-in commands.
+
+Qtile is written and configured entirely in Python, which means you can
+leverage the full power and flexibility of the language to make it fit your
+needs.
+
+WWW: http://qtile.org
